extension.description: Batch-format files in a workspace, folder, by glob pattern, or across just your Git changes. Includes a configuration & log webview panel, .gitignore support, dry-run preview, and per-file result reporting.
config.title: Format Files
config.logLevel.description: Severity threshold for the **Format Files** output channel. Messages below this level are suppressed.
config.logLevel.debug: Verbose tracing for debugging the extension itself.
config.logLevel.warn: Recoverable issues (a single file failed to format).
config.extensionsToInclude.description: Comma-separated list of file extensions to include (e.g. `ts,tsx,js,json`). The leading dot is optional. Leave empty to include every file under the chosen scope.
config.excludedFolders.description: Folder names (or workspace-relative paths) to exclude. Each entry is converted to the glob `**/<name>/**`.
config.inheritWorkspaceExcludedFiles.des: Merge enabled keys from the workspace `files.exclude` setting into the exclude pattern.
config.runOrganizeImports.description: Run **Organize Imports** on each file before formatting.
config.useGitIgnore.description: Skip files matched by any `.gitignore` in the workspace tree.
config.gitScope.includeStaged.descriptio: When `true`, the **Run on Git changes** command processes files in the index (staged via `git add`).
config.gitScope.includeModified.descript: When `true`, the **Run on Git changes** command processes tracked files with unstaged modifications.
config.gitScope.includeUntracked.descrip: When `true`, the **Run on Git changes** command processes new files Git has not yet tracked.
config.dryRunFirst.description: When `true`, every Run shows a confirmation panel listing the matched files, extension breakdown, and ETA before any file is touched.
config.locale.description: Language used inside the Format Files panel. `auto` follows VSCode's display language. (Command palette and settings UI always follow VSCode.)
